# MeuralMCP

> MeuralMCP is a Python daemon, REST API, and hosted MCP service for locally
> managing Netgear Meural Canvas preview images. It polls frames because
> previews can be lost after sleep or boot, stores one assigned image per
> device, validates image orientation, and exposes MCP tools for coding agents.

This repository is intended to be public and generic. Do not add real device
names, IP addresses, cloud IDs, serial numbers, MAC addresses, API tokens,
passwords, cookies, private images, or generated runtime state.

## Primary Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): user setup, cloud init, service samples, HTTPS reverse proxy guidance, FAQ, and credits.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): contributor and coding-agent safety rules for keeping the repo public-safe.
- [examples/config.example.json](examples/config.example.json): generic configuration shape.
- [docs/sleep-schedules.md](docs/sleep-schedules.md): optional per-device sleep window behavior.

## Runtime Surfaces

- [src/meural_mcp/cli.py](src/meural_mcp/cli.py): command-line entrypoint for init, daemon, REST API, and MCP server.
- [src/meural_mcp/manager.py](src/meural_mcp/manager.py): core device config, image assignment, preview reload, reachability, blank gallery, and timeout logic.
- [src/meural_mcp/mcp_server.py](src/meural_mcp/mcp_server.py): MCP tools for listing devices, reading status, reading assigned image paths, and setting current images.
- [src/meural_mcp/api.py](src/meural_mcp/api.py): token-protected REST API and hosted remote MCP endpoint at `/mcp/`.
- [src/meural_mcp/cloud.py](src/meural_mcp/cloud.py): Meural cloud authentication and setup calls.
- [src/meural_mcp/local.py](src/meural_mcp/local.py): local frame `/remote` calls.

## Deployment Examples

- [examples/systemd/meural-mcp-daemon.service](examples/systemd/meural-mcp-daemon.service): system daemon sample.
- [examples/systemd/meural-mcp-api.service](examples/systemd/meural-mcp-api.service): localhost REST API service sample.
- [examples/systemd/meural-mcp-user.service](examples/systemd/meural-mcp-user.service): user-level daemon sample.
- [examples/reverse-proxy/Caddyfile](examples/reverse-proxy/Caddyfile): Caddy HTTPS reverse proxy sample.
- [examples/reverse-proxy/nginx.conf](examples/reverse-proxy/nginx.conf): nginx HTTPS reverse proxy sample.

## MCP Client Model

Preferred: run the daemon/API on the service host and expose remote MCP at
`/mcp/` over HTTPS. Clients that support remote MCP should connect to that URL
with the shared bearer token and should not need this package installed locally.

For hosted remote MCP, use `set_device_image_data` to upload image bytes as
base64. `set_device_image` takes a path on the machine running the MCP server,
so it is mainly for stdio adapters or server-local files.

Fallback: run `meural-mcp mcp --transport stdio` locally only for clients that
cannot speak remote MCP. The stdio adapter may point at a remote REST API with
`MEURAL_MCP_API_URL`.

## Tests

- [tests/test_manager.py](tests/test_manager.py): manager behavior, image validation, cloud setup, and reachability.
- [tests/test_mcp_server.py](tests/test_mcp_server.py): MCP tool behavior and image-write error contracts.
- [tests/test_cli.py](tests/test_cli.py): CLI init and systemd helper behavior.
- [tests/test_api.py](tests/test_api.py): REST API token authentication.
